name: nanobind (Next-Gen C++ Python Bindings)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); from pybind11 original author

performance.notes: 4x smaller binaries, 2x faster compilation vs pybind11; comparable runtime performance

agent_readiness.notes: Next-generation C++ Python bindings library by the original pybind11 author. Design goals: smaller binary sizes (4x), faster compile times (2x), and a cleaner internals model. Compatible API with pybind11 but not drop-in replacement. NumPy/PyTorch tensor integration. Python 3.8+. Used by Google JAX, PyTorch, and other major projects. Preferred over pybind11 for new projects requiring C++ Python bindings.

gotchas: Not a drop-in replacement for pybind11 (requires migration effort)
Less community content than pybind11 (newer project)
Fewer third-party examples compared to pybind11
best_when: New C++ Python extension projects (prefer over pybind11)
When binary size or compile time matters
High-performance Python extensions for numerical computing
avoid_when: When pure Python performance tools (Numba) suffice
summary: nanobind is the next-generation C++ Python bindings library from the pybind11 author. 4x smaller binaries and 2x faster compilation than pybind11. Used by Google JAX and PyTorch. Not a drop-in pybind11 replacement but the preferred choice for new C++ Python extension projects needing NumPy/tensor integration.
